On this page

Curriculum Overview ​

Welcome to the XBot programming curriculum. By the end, you'll be able to write robot code that drives, shoots, and scores.

Learning Path ​

Getting Started ​

ModuleTopicTime
1. Environment SetupInstall Java, VSCode, WPILib, Git30 min
2. Java BasicsVariables, methods, classes, interfaces45 min
3. Git & GitHubClone, commit, branch, pull requests30 min

Robot Fundamentals ​

ModuleTopicTime
4. Robot ArchitectureHow XBot code is organized20 min
5. Electrical ContractWiring definitions as code20 min
6. Motor ControlControlling motors safely30 min
7. PID LogicProportional-Integral-Derivative control30 min

XBot Patterns ​

ModuleTopicTime
8. Dependency InjectionDagger and automatic wiring30 min
9. Providers & FactoriesCreating objects with runtime params20 min
10. Command-BasedWPILib command framework30 min
11. MaintainersContinuous PID maintenance25 min
12. Swerve DriveHolonomic driving40 min
13. Properties & TuningRuntime-tunable values15 min

Practice Repo ​

All exercises use XbotEdu - a practice robot project with unit tests so you can code without a physical robot.

bash
git clone https://github.com/Team488/XbotEdu.git
cd XbotEdu
./gradlew test
Edit this page on GitHub
Pager
Next pageEnvironment Setup

Built for XBot Robotics Team 488
